title: Gilead Axes Phase III Trial in Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis
summary: Gilead Sciences is halting its Phase III trial of ambrisentan (Letairis) in patients with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) after an interim Data Monitoring Committee analysis found no evidence of treatment benefit. The decision reinforces what analysts describe as IPF being a 'graveyard' for clinical trials, following similar failures by Actelion's Tracleer and highlighting the high barriers to entry for drug development in this disease area.
analysis_notes: Article is from 2010-2011 based on date references (Q3 2009 sales, 2011 study timelines). Historical content discussing IPF drug development challenges; low clinical actionability today but useful for context on the competitive landscape in pulmonary fibrosis.
